bead.title: graphql: expose needs-human beads and review resolution
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The web GUI cannot implement a Needs review filter or submit operator review decisions without GraphQL support. Existing bead queries and mutations expose generic lifecycle operations but not needs-human metadata or explicit review resolution.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:169-179 defines BeadStatusCounts without needsHuman or workerReady. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2263-2329 defines bead queries but no beadsNeedsHuman query. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3153-3188 defines bead mutations but no beadHumanResolve mutation. cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:112-156 provides generic beadUpdate but not action-specific resolution with required notes.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend GraphQL with needs-human fields on Bead, needsHuman/workerReady counts, beadsNeedsHuman query, and beadHumanResolve mutation using action enum retry|split|obsolete|defer, required note, and optional children. Resolvers delegate to bead package helpers shared with CLI.

NON-SCOPE
Frontend rendering, execute-loop transition changes, or full artifact review chat.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-c5cab746.
bead.acceptance:
1. schema.graphql exposes Bead.needsHuman, Bead.needsHumanReason, Bead.needsHumanSuggestedAction, Bead.needsHumanSummary, BeadStatusCounts.needsHuman, BeadStatusCounts.workerReady, beadsNeedsHuman, and beadHumanResolve.
2. TestGraphQLBeadsNeedsHumanQuery verifies needs-human rows include reason/source/suggested action and exclude ordinary open beads.
3. TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRetry verifies retry resolution removes needs_human and appends a resolution event.
4. TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRequiresNote verifies all resolve actions reject empty notes.
5. TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsNeedsHuman verifies counts match store.Status().
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

{"rationale":"Criterion 1 (title): PASS — 'expose' is imperative, subsystem and change named. Criterion 2 (root cause with file:line): PASS — four precise file:line anchors in schema.graphql and resolver_mutation_beads.go. Criterion 3 (numbered AC with Test* names): PASS — AC 2–5 each name a distinct TestGraphQL* function. Criterion 4 (wired-in assertion): PASS — TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRequiresNote covers the empty-note constraint. Criterion 5 (go test + lefthook gate): PASS — AC 6 and 7 present verbatim. Criterion 6 (labels with cross-refs): PASS — phase:2, area:*, kind:feature, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010 all present. Criterion 7 (parent + deps): FAIL — parent ddx-ac10e7af is declared, but the dep on ddx-c5cab746 lives only in description prose; it must be a formal dependency entry so the scheduler can block on it. Criterion 8 (standalone description): PASS — no /tmp refs, no chat context required. Operational note: every execution attempt shows resolved_provider='' and routing_intent_degraded=true, and the lint hook repeatedly warns missing-harness. The bead has no harness pin, so the executor cannot route it and falls through to no_changes. This is not a rubric criterion but is the proximate cause of execute-loop-no-changes-count:4 and must be fixed before the next drain attempt.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Promote the dep on ddx-c5cab746 from description prose to a formal deps field (e.g. add it via `ddx bead dep add ddx-a06ef04a ddx-c5cab746`) so the scheduler blocks correctly.","Add a harness pin (e.g. `harness: claude` in the bead's custom_fields or via a `--harness` flag in the dispatch config) to resolve the persistent missing-harness routing failure that has caused four consecutive no_changes executions."],"waivers_applied":[]}
